name: Molasses (cane, blackstrap-type) (food medium)
basis: per 100 g as-consumed (approx. 78 g dry matter, 22 g water)

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Measured sucrose in molasses; the single largest fermentable carbon source.

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Measured fructose (invert sugar) in molasses.

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Measured dextrose/glucose (invert sugar) in molasses.

components.name: total sugars
components.evidence: Total-sugar assay; exceeds the summed sucrose+glucose+fructose (54.1 g), the remainder reflecting additional/unresolved sugars (e.g. raffinose, incomplete hydrolysis fractions). Reported for completeness; do not double-count with the individual sugars.

components.name: lactic acid
components.source: Palmonari et al. 2020 J Dairy Sci doi:10.3168/jds.2019-17644
components.evidence: Cane molasses mean 6.1% of DM (range 1.6-12.8% DM); ~78% DM -> ~4.8 g/100g. A dominant organic-acid carbon/energy source, absent from the FooDB record.

components.name: aconitic acid
components.source: Palmonari et al. 2020 J Dairy Sci doi:10.3168/jds.2019-17644
components.evidence: Cane-molasses signature acid, 1.42% of DM (found only in cane, not beet); ~1.1 g/100g. Missing from FooDB record.

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Palmonari et al. 2020 J Dairy Sci doi:10.3168/jds.2019-17644; Olbrich 'The Molasses'
components.evidence: Present but minor in cane molasses (precursor of aconitic acid); not individually quantified here.

components.name: malic acid
components.source: Palmonari et al. 2020 J Dairy Sci doi:10.3168/jds.2019-17644
components.evidence: Detected but minor/trace in both cane and beet molasses; unquantified.

components.name: acetic acid
components.source: Palmonari et al. 2020 J Dairy Sci doi:10.3168/jds.2019-17644
components.evidence: Minor volatile acid in molasses; unquantified.

components.name: aspartic acid
components.source: Mee 1979 J Sci Food Agric doi:10.1002/jsfa.2740300413; review PMC7827330
components.evidence: Aspartate is a major free amino acid of cane molasses, ~7.6-17.8 g/kg DM (midpoint ~12.7 g/kg DM -> ~1.0 g/100g).

components.name: glutamic acid
components.evidence: ~7.6 g/kg DM -> ~0.59 g/100g; second major free amino acid.

components.name: gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA)
components.source: Mee 1979 J Sci Food Agric doi:10.1002/jsfa.2740300413
components.evidence: Detected among free amino acids of cane juice/molasses; unquantified.

components.name: betaine
components.evidence: Nitrogenous osmolyte, 0.5-0.7% of molasses (higher in beet, present in cane); usable N/methyl-donor source. Absent from FooDB record.

components.name: crude protein / amino nitrogen
components.source: review PMC7827330; dellait comparison of Palmonari data
components.evidence: Cane molasses crude protein ~6.7% of DM (~4.5-5 g/100g), largely free amino acids and non-protein N (betaine); note USDA lists Kjeldahl protein ~0 g because most N is non-protein.

components.name: ammonium
components.evidence: Part of the non-protein soluble nitrogen fraction; present at low levels, unquantified.

components.name: potassium
components.evidence: Dominant mineral of molasses.

components.name: magnesium
components.evidence: Measured Mg.

components.name: calcium
components.evidence: Measured Ca.

components.name: sodium
components.evidence: Measured Na.

components.name: phosphate (as P)
components.evidence: Measured phosphorus.

components.name: iron
components.evidence: Measured Fe; consolidates the FooDB duplicate iron species (Iron, Fe2+, Fe3+).

components.name: manganese
components.evidence: Measured Mn.

components.name: copper
components.evidence: Measured Cu.

components.name: zinc
components.evidence: Measured Zn.

components.name: selenium
components.evidence: Measured Se.

components.name: sulfate (as S)
components.source: review PMC7827330 (S ~8.2 g/kg DM); FooDB FOOD00786
components.evidence: Cane molasses is sulfate-rich (~0.6-0.8 g S/100g); genuine anion, kept but not pinned to a single USDA value.

components.name: chloride
components.source: dellait/Palmonari (cane > beet in chlorides); FooDB FOOD00786
components.evidence: Present; genuine electrolyte, unquantified here.

components.name: molybdate (molybdenum)
components.evidence: Trace micronutrient; kept at trace, unquantified.

components.name: cobalt
components.evidence: Genuine trace micronutrient of molasses (B12 cofactor); trace, unquantified.

components.name: vitamin B6 (pyridoxine)
components.evidence: Molasses is notably B6-rich.

components.name: pantothenic acid (B5)
components.evidence: Measured B5.

components.name: niacin (B3, nicotinate)
components.evidence: Measured B3.

components.name: thiamine (B1)
components.evidence: Measured B1.

components.name: riboflavin (B2)
components.evidence: Measured B2 (low in USDA; literature gives ~2.6 mg/kg DM).

components.name: biotin (B7)
components.evidence: ~0.68 mg/kg DM -> ~0.053 mg/100g; growth-relevant vitamin, absent from FooDB record.

components.name: choline
components.evidence: Measured choline.

components.name: water
components.evidence: Matrix water; molasses is ~22% water / ~78% dry matter.

remove.name: CO2 CO2
remove.reason: Dissolved gas / system species, not a growth nutrient of molasses

remove.name: O2 O2
remove.reason: Dissolved gas / system species, not a food nutrient

remove.name: H+
remove.reason: Proton/pH bookkeeping species, not a nutrient

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: Non-nutritive trace element / FooDB contaminant, not a defined molasses nutrient

remove.name: Fe2+ mitochondria
remove.reason: Duplicate/mislabeled iron species (organelle-tagged); consolidated into a single iron entry

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+)
remove.reason: Duplicate iron species; consolidated into a single iron entry

missing_added: lactic acid (~4.8 g/100g, dominant organic acid)
quantitative amounts for sucrose, glucose, fructose and all minerals/vitamins
keep_uncovered: betaine (may lack a BiGG met in some models)
notes: Cane (blackstrap-type) molasses. Dominant carbon/energy sources: sucrose (~29 g/100g) plus invert sugars glucose+fructose (~25 g/100g), with organic acids lactic (~4.8 g/100g) and aconitic (~1.1 g/100g) as major secondary substrates that were entirely missing from the FooDB record. High mineral load (K, Mg, Ca) and B-vitamins (B6, B5, B3). Amino-acid and organic-acid amounts are converted from % dry matter (~78% DM) of peer-reviewed cane-molasses datasets; USDA FDC 168820 supplies the sugar/mineral/vitamin panel.
